title: Cantinoa colombiana
taxonomy: Cantinoa colombiana is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family is commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. The genus Cantinoa itself is a part of the Lamiaceae family and is native to South America. Cantinoa colombiana is often placed in the subfamily Nepetoideae, a subfamily within the Lamiaceae family. Cantinoa colombiana is a species of flowering plant that is part of the larger classification of Lamiaceae.

intro: Cantinoa colombiana, also known as Colombian marigold, is an herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as antioxidant and antiviral effects. The bioactive constituents of Cantinoa colombiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cantinoa colombiana is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
